metadata.title:
RUSH FOR CANDY LIQUOR DROPS


STARTS W. c. 7. IT, CRUSADE

pages.raw_text:
Special Correspondence to The Press


NEW YORK, Oct. Boys, girls
and young women are threatened
with "a danger, the limits of which
are almost incalculable,' according
to Mrs. Helen J. Andrus, president
of the New York County w. G.
T. u, and Health Commissioner
Darlington.


The recent discovery by Mrs. An
drus and Darlington that intoxi-
cants are being sold over the coun-
ters of confectionery stores and
chocolate shops has raised a storm
about the heads of the purveyors of
sweets, and started crusade in
which the temperance leader is
backed by the metropolitan police.


The discovery, while new to Mrs.
Andrus and possibly to the present
generation, is really old. 1t is the
concealing of liquor in chocolates
and other candies. Not much of a
drink is obtained from one choco-
late drop, but pound of the liquor
lied sweets will furnish almost any
one a lot of exhiliratiOn.


They call it the candy jag here.
. For some time past the chocolate
shops have been selling Jamaica
rum chocolates, whistle candies, alb
sinthe drops. creme de menthe
plums and wine drops. Girls en-
plOyed in downtown offices crowded
the shops during the noon hour,
and the places did an amazing busl-
ness. One shop near Wall street
created such sensation that the
